title: adinandra collina
intro: Adinandra collina, also known as the waxvine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark and leaves, which are traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent infections. Adinandra collina can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infused tea, making it a versatile herbal remedy that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
